label: Applicability Dimensions
description: Canonical dimensions used to determine whether evidence fits the target execution context.
usageNotes: Score or adjudicate each dimension separately before computing overall applicability.
A single failed dimension can trigger local review even when others fit.

entries.label: Population Fit
entries.description: Fit between source population and target patient population.
usageNotes: Compare age, diagnosis, severity, biomarkers and comorbidity profile.

entries.label: Intervention Fit
entries.description: Fit between source intervention and target intervention as implemented.
usageNotes: Include route, device generation, technique and comparator mismatch.

entries.label: Setting Fit
entries.description: Fit between source care setting and target operational setting.
usageNotes: Important across ambulatory, ward, ICU and tertiary center contexts.

entries.label: Jurisdiction Fit
entries.description: Fit between source governance/regulatory context and target jurisdiction.
usageNotes: Required for execution legality and reimbursement sensitivity.

entries.label: Resource Fit
entries.description: Fit between source-required resources and target available resources.
usageNotes: A failed resource fit should alter readiness, not clinical truth itself.
